title: Aeranthes lindenii
taxonomy: Aeranthes lindenii is a species of orchid in the genus Aeranthes and family Orchidaceae. This genus, Aeranthes, falls under the subfamily Angraecinae. Aeranthes lindenii is further classified under the subtribe Aeranthinae. It belongs to the order Asparagales and the division Magnoliophyta. Aeranthes lindenii is commonly known as the 'Linden's Aeranthes' and is native to Madagascar.

intro: Aeranthes lindenii, also known as the 'Queen of the night', is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. Aeranthes lindenii is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, including Africa and Asia.
